title: USD Composition Provenance Capability
purpose: Define the runtime representation for strength-ordered contributor provenance carried by composed namespace sources. The contract prevents generated providers from storing USD object/spec paths as composite strings such as asset.usda:/Prim.

included: source or layer identifiers paired with spec paths
strong-to-weak contributing opinion records returned by composition namespace sources
summary records for composition arcs that include USD object/spec paths
adapter conversion of typed provenance into legacy conformance strings
deferred: requiring a particular source/layer identifier type
requiring a particular C++ struct name, ABI layout, allocator, or ownership model
resource identity semantics beyond not confusing resource identifiers with USD object paths
full opinion stacks with per-field strength metadata

model.SourceIdentifier: A target-native or string-backed identifier for a layer, asset, resource, or synthetic source. This is not a USD object path.
model.SpecPath: A semantic USD path handle/reference identifying the contributing prim, property, variant, or other spec path inside the source.
model.ContributingOpinion: A target-native record containing at least a source identifier and semantic spec path, ordered strong-to-weak by the provider.
model.BoundaryContributionString: A diagnostic, JSON, report, or ABI rendering such as source.usda:/World produced from a typed ContributingOpinion at the boundary.

laws: composition providers that expose contributingSources, contributing opinions, or equivalent provenance must store each contributor as a typed record, not as one raw string
the USD object/spec path component of a contributor must be a SemanticPath value or equivalent target-native path reference
source identifiers may be strings, resource identifiers, layer handles, or target-native source handles, but they must not be treated as USD object paths unless the authored field type is path-valued
generated domain logic must not concatenate source identifiers and paths into strings for later equality, lookup, prefix checks, path extraction, or composition decisions
generated domain logic must not parse contributor display strings to recover paths for validation or composition; it must compare the typed source identifier and semantic path fields directly

providers that expose flattened NamespaceSource inputs for testing or stage population must still preserve enough separable per-family provenance or family-only opinion views for downstream composition providers that need to ignore one family while consuming another
composition arc summary records with primPath, sourcePath, targetPath, variantPath, or mapped contributor paths must store those path fields as SemanticPath values in domain code
adapter JSON, diagnostics, text reports, and C ABI outputs may render ContributingOpinion records as strings, but those strings are boundary representations
existing conformance fields named contributingSources may remain string arrays in adapter JSON if they are rendered from typed ContributingOpinion records
